title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, and Status Contracts · Mastery Check
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for http semantics, resources, methods, and status contracts and requires the smallest safe resource, method, status, and retry semantics table with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for resource, method, status, and retry semantics table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.
Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method semantics independently and preserve them through handler behavior.
Choose status, Location, validators, and response content for create, replace, partial update, asynchronous work, and deletion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
